new_classes.label_en: Liqueur in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22087090)
new_classes.label_de: Likör in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22087090)
new_classes.definition_en: Liqueurs and cordials presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 70 90 (Chapter 22). Liqueurs are spirituous beverages with added sugar, sweetening matter, and flavouring, as described in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, first paragraph (B) and third paragraph.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criterion for CN 22087090 — liqueurs and cordials in containers of more than 2 litres — is captured via the subclass relationship under eucn:Liqueur, the cnHeadingCode hasValue restriction, and the container volume lower bound (> 2 L). The disjointness with LiqueurSmallContainer (≤ 2 L, CN 22087010) is expressed via the complement restriction. A minor gap remains: the legal note references the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, first paragraph (B) and third paragraph, which elaborates on the qualitative definition of liqueurs (added sugar, sweetening matter, flavouring, minimum sugar content). These fine-grained compositional criteria (e.g. minimum sugar content threshold) are not fully axiomatised in the current TBox, leading to a slight reduction in coverage.
